title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Delayed Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Contract Lens: Quorum Priority Levels
summary: A design copies old TTL dead-letter retry loops into RabbitMQ 4.3, uses priority as unlimited preemption, leaves consumer timeouts unrelated to handler deadlines, and assumes new broker behavior exists on older clusters. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quorum priority levels increment in RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Quorum Priority Levels · Theory Quorum Priority Levels
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 3111 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Quorum Priority Levels contract for the supplied data.
